turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some issues with my stomach for a few weeks now. I get crampy pain, mostly on the left side, and I've had diarrhea like 8-10 times a day, it's really loose and watery.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is pretty constant, but it gets worse when I have a bowel movement. The diarrhea is what's been really affecting my life, though – it's hard to focus at work and I feel exhausted all the time.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 2 weeks ago, but looking back, I think the cramps were there a bit before that. The diarrhea definitely kicked in around 10 days ago, and it's been non-stop since then.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens every single day, at least 8-10 times a day, usually after meals or when I'm feeling stressed. The frequency is constant; it hasn't decreased even with loperamide.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 in terms of severity, mainly because I'm so tired all the time and it's affecting my daily life. The pain itself is more like a 6 or 7, but the frequency and urgency of the bowel movements are what really get to me.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I feel a bit of urgency with each bowel movement, like I have to go right away. And sometimes, after I've had a particularly bad episode, I might get a little lightheaded or dizzy from losing so much fluid, but that's about it. The crampy pain is usually the only other symptom I experience.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yeah, definitely. It gets a lot worse when I'm stressed or under pressure at work. I've been putting in 60+ hour weeks and it feels like my body is just not handling it well. And even though I try to eat regular meals, sometimes going to restaurants or eating takeout seems to make the diarrhea worse too.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I do get a bit lightheaded or dizzy after really bad episodes, especially when I've lost a lot of fluid. Other than that, no, I don't usually notice any sweating or nausea.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it mostly stays in my lower abdomen and doesn't radiate up into my back or anywhere else. The cramping is pretty localized to that area, which is what makes it so frustrating because I feel like it's taking over my daily life with its frequency and severity.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really noticed any swelling in my legs or feet. My main concern is the constant diarrhea and how it's affecting my daily life and work performance.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been self-medicating with loperamide, Imodium, for the past couple of weeks because I thought that would help with the diarrhea. It hasn't really made a significant difference, though.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mom is healthy and doesn't have any chronic conditions that I know of. As for me, no, I don't think I do – I've been in good health until this started happening with my stomach a couple weeks ago.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about that, to be honest. I haven't really thought about getting my cholesterol checked recently. My main concern right now is figuring out what's going on with my stomach and why this diarrhea isn't stopping. Can we focus on that for a bit?

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do have a few beers most nights, but I've actually increased it to 4-5 per night over the past month because of stress from my symptoms and work. It's not something I normally do, but I figured maybe it was just stress relief or something.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet hasn't been great lately due to working long hours. I've mostly been eating takeout or grabbing something quick because I don't have time for regular meals. Exercise-wise, I try to go for a run or do some yoga on the weekends, but during the week, it's tough to find the energy to do much of anything physical after work.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. My dad has type 2 diabetes, but I'm not aware of any heart disease or heart attacks in my family. Actually, it makes me think of something - my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, and that's been on my mind lately because of my own symptoms.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30. That's actually something I've been worried about lately, given my own symptoms and the fact that I'm only 25 now.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: To be honest, it's been over two years since I last visited a doctor. I've been pretty healthy for most of my life, and with work getting busier, I just haven't had the chance to prioritize going in for regular check-ups or addressing any potential issues when they started. But now that this has been going on for so long, I'm definitely feeling like it's time to get some professional help and figure out what's causing these symptoms.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I've been self-medicating with loperamide for a couple of weeks now, but I haven't noticed any allergic reactions or issues from taking it.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've actually been experiencing some unintentional weight loss over the past few weeks - about 4 kg (9 lbs) in total. And to be honest, it's really affecting my energy levels and work performance. The stress from these symptoms is also causing me a lot of anxiety and frustration, which I'm trying to manage with more beer than usual, but like I said, that's not something I normally do.
